activities_projects.title: Atmosphere Layer Cake
activities_projects.objective: Identify the five layers of the atmosphere
steps: Draw five horizontal sections starting from the ground up
Label them: Troposphere, Stratosphere, Mesosphere, Thermosphere, and Exosphere
Draw a small airplane in the Stratosphere and a weather balloon in the Troposphere
Color each layer differently to show they are separate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Memorizes the sequence of atmospheric layers from the Earth's surface
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct naming and ordering of all five layers (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Air Composition Pie Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn the percentage of different gases in the air
steps: Divide the paper plate into sections representing different gases
Color 78% of the plate Blue for Nitrogen
Color 21% Red for Oxygen
Color the tiny remaining 1% Green for Argon, $CO_2$, and others
Label each section with its name and percentage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes Nitrogen and Oxygen as the most abundant gases in the atmosphere
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the proportions and labeling (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Greenhouse Effect Jar
activities_projects.objective: Understand how Carbon Dioxide traps heat
steps: Place one thermometer inside a glass jar and seal it with a lid
Keep the second thermometer in the open air next to the jar
Leave both in the sun for 30 minutes
Compare the temperatures and note which one is higher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains how trapped air stays warmer, similar to the Greenhouse Effect
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct recording of temperature difference and basic reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Paper Strip Wind Vane
activities_projects.objective: Identify the direction of the wind
steps: Attach a paper strip to one end of a straw
Pin the middle of the straw to the pencil eraser so it can spin freely
Hold it outside and see where the paper strip points
Note that wind is named after the direction from which it blows
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that wind is the movement of air and has a specific direction
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful construction and observation of wind direction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Air Pressure Bottle Trick
steps: Try to blow up a balloon inside an empty plastic bottle
Observe that it is hard to do because air inside the bottle takes up space
Poke a small hole in the bottom of the bottle and try again
Watch the balloon inflate as air escapes through the hole
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that air occupies space and exerts pressure
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the balloon inflated only after the hole was made (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rain Gauge Maker
steps: Cut the top off a plastic bottle and invert it like a funnel into the base
Tape a ruler to the side or mark 1 cm intervals with a marker
Place it in an open area when it rains
Check how many centimeters of water were collected after the rain stops
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the standard method for measuring precipitation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the scale and recorded rainfall measurement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Weather Diary
activities_projects.objective: Record daily changes in the atmosphere
steps: For 5 days, record the weather every morning
Note: Temperature (Hot/Cold), Sky (Clear/Cloudy), and Wind (Breezy/Still)
Identify if it is a sunny day or a rainy day
Discuss if weather stays the same every day
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of the 5-day record and observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cloud in a Jar
steps: Pour a little hot water into the jar and swirl it to warm the sides
Place the metal lid upside down on top of the jar
Place ice cubes on the lid
Watch the 'mist' or cloud form inside the jar near the top
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the process of water vapor turning into liquid droplets (Condensation)
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful observation of the cloud and explanation of the role of ice (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Oxygen and Burning
activities_projects.objective: Prove that air is needed for burning
steps: Light a candle and fix it on a table
Cover the burning candle with an inverted glass tumbler
Observe how long it takes for the flame to go out
Discuss which gas was used up by the flame
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies Oxygen as the gas that supports combustion
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct reasoning for why the candle stopped burning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Warming Poster
activities_projects.objective: Raise awareness about rising Earth temperatures
steps: Draw a picture of the Earth wearing a scarf or looking hot
Draw cars and factories releasing smoke ($CO_2$)
Write a simple message: 'Stop Global Warming' or 'Plant More Trees'
List one way trees help (by giving Oxygen and taking $CO_2$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the human impact on the atmosphere's balance
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of the environmental message (10 marks)
